intro: This week prioritises build strength without losing fitness across 4 training days using the 4-day strength priority structure.
The structure is built to prioritising strength development first, while keeping conditioning in to maintain fitness and athleticism, while still matching your current setup (full gym access).
With moderate training time available, the week balances quality and recovery so you can progress without feeling overloaded.
Because you’re still building structure, the week keeps complexity under control and focuses on repeatable, manageable sessions.
Running guidance: Walk-run, easy volume, and technique build the engine — intensity comes after the habits stick.
Execute this week by controlling easy work, hitting quality sessions with intent, and respecting recovery between hard days.

schedule.intent: Low-stress recovery support.

schedule.title: Knee-Friendly Lower Strength
schedule.intent: Primary lower-body strength day.

warm_up: 8–10 min easy bike or brisk walk
main: Step-Down off low box — 3 × 8 each side — only range that stays symptom-free
Calf raise + seated tib raise superset — 3 rounds moderate effort
notes: Purpose: Maintain lower-body strength without forcing angry knees through spicy ranges.
This strength session is included to make sure the week is still driving muscle and strength forward, not just burning calories.
Execution focus: If a rep sharpens symptoms, shorten range immediately — chase control, not load.
Effort guide: 6-7/10
Pain-free range only on every pattern — reduce load before changing technique.

priority.reason: This is one of the main goal-driving sessions for the week.

intro: This week prioritises build strength without losing fitness across 5 training days using the 5-day strength priority structure.
The structure is built to prioritising strength development first, while keeping conditioning in to maintain fitness and athleticism, while still matching your current setup (full gym access).
With a solid weekly training window, we can include enough quality, support work, and aerobic volume to make the week feel complete.
Because you already have a base of structured training, the week can push quality while still staying recoverable.
Running guidance: Walk-run, easy volume, and technique build the engine — intensity comes after the habits stick.
Execute this week by controlling easy work, hitting quality sessions with intent, and respecting recovery between hard days.

schedule.title: Lower Strength (Posterior Chain)
schedule.intent: Primary lower-body strength day.

finish: 3 rounds — 15 KB swings + 10 reverse lunges
notes: Purpose: Build hinge strength and posterior-chain resilience.
This strength session is included to make sure the week is still driving muscle and strength forward, not just burning calories.
Execution focus: Push hips back and keep tension throughout.
Effort guide: 7-8/10
Great for runners and Hyrox athletes needing stronger posterior chain output.

priority.label: priority_1
priority.reason: This is one of the main goal-driving sessions for the week.

schedule.title: Upper Full Strength
schedule.intent: Upper-body strength and support.

notes: Purpose: Cover all major upper patterns when only one upper day exists.
This strength session is included to make sure the week is still driving muscle and strength forward, not just burning calories.
Execution focus: Hit full range and leave 1–2 reps in reserve.
Effort guide: 6-7/10
This is non-negotiable when upper frequency is only once per week.

priority.label: priority_1
priority.reason: This is one of the main goal-driving sessions for the week.

schedule.title: Run-Walk Aerobic Base
schedule.intent: Entry-level quality run that keeps structure without excess fatigue.

main: 8 rounds: 2 min easy run + 1 min walk
notes: Purpose: Build beginner aerobic volume safely with controlled impact.
This session plays a specific role in balancing quality, recovery, and performance across the week.
Execution focus: Stay conversational and keep every run segment smooth.
Effort guide: 4/10
Pace guide: based on your submitted 5km, easy aerobic work sits around 7:10–8:00/km. Use RPE and control first.
If form fades, shorten run reps and keep quality high.

priority.label: priority_2
priority.reason: This supports the key work without carrying the same priority.

schedule.title: Bodyweight Strength Base
schedule.intent: Condensed full-body strength coverage.

notes: Purpose: Build movement quality and basic strength without equipment.
This strength session is included to make sure the week is still driving muscle and strength forward, not just burning calories.
Execution focus: Pause where you feel sloppy — quality is the load.
Effort guide: 5-6/10
Quality first, effort second.

priority.label: priority_1
priority.reason: This is one of the main goal-driving sessions for the week.

schedule.title: Outdoor Walk-Run Aerobic
schedule.intent: Low-cost aerobic support that fits around harder sessions.

notes: Purpose: Build aerobic consistency without needing gym equipment.
This session plays a specific role in balancing quality, recovery, and performance across the week.
Execution focus: Stay conversational — if you can’t speak in short sentences, ease off.
Effort guide: 4/10
Pace guide: based on your submitted 5km, easy aerobic work sits around 7:10–8:00/km. Use RPE and control first.
Keep this conversational.

priority.label: priority_2
priority.reason: This supports the key work without carrying the same priority.

main: 20–40 min easy Z2 bike or jog
notes: Stay controlled and conversational throughout.

intro: This week prioritises build strength without losing fitness across 5 training days using the 5-day strength priority structure.
The structure is built to prioritising strength development first, while keeping conditioning in to maintain fitness and athleticism, while still matching your current setup (full gym access).
Because you have more training time and are open to doubles, the structure can include extra aerobic support without compromising the main sessions.
Because your training level is more advanced, the week can handle slightly more density, sharper quality, and more demanding session pairings.
Running guidance: Emphasise aerobic strength and controlled quality; avoid stacking hard days without purpose.
Execute this week by controlling easy work, hitting quality sessions with intent, and respecting recovery between hard days.

schedule.title: Lower Full Strength
schedule.intent: Primary lower-body strength day.

finish: Optional: 20–30 min Z2 bike after lifting
Optional second session: 20–30 min easy Z2 bike

This strength session is included to make sure the week is still driving muscle and strength forward, not just burning calories.
Execution focus: Control every rep and keep good positions.

priority.reason: This is one of the main goal-driving sessions for the week.

finish: Optional: 20–30 min Z2 bike after lifting

This strength session is included to make sure the week is still driving muscle and strength forward, not just burning calories.

finish: Optional: 20–30 min Z2 bike after lifting

intro: This week prioritises improve hybrid / hyrox performance across 4 training days using the 4-day short time structure.

The structure is built to prioritising threshold work, compromised efforts, and supportive strength to improve how well you perform under fatigue, while still matching your current setup (full gym access).
With moderate training time available, the week balances quality and recovery so you can progress without feeling overloaded.
Because you’re still building structure, the week keeps complexity under control and focuses on repeatable, manageable sessions.
Running guidance: Walk-run, easy volume, and technique build the engine — intensity comes after the habits stick.
Execute this week by controlling easy work, hitting quality sessions with intent, and respecting recovery between hard days.

schedule.title: Muscle Maintenance for Runners
schedule.intent: Condensed full-body strength coverage.

notes: Purpose: Maintain muscle and strength while keeping legs fresh for running.
This strength work supports performance by building positions, force output, and durability.
Execution focus: Leave 2–3 reps in reserve — you should finish feeling springier, not sluggish.
Effort guide: 6-7/10
Leave the gym feeling better, not destroyed.

priority.label: priority_3
priority.reason: This is useful support work, but it is the easiest to flex if life gets busy.

Keep posture tall and breathing controlled.

Execution focus: Conversation-speed running only — walk when quality dips.

Stay conversational and controlled.

Optional: 20–30 min Z2 bike after lifting
notes: Purpose: Build athletic pressing and pulling with dynamic output.
This strength work supports performance by building positions, force output, and durability.
Execution focus: Move explosively but stay tidy.
Effort guide: 6-7/10
Useful for athletes who want upper strength with some engine crossover.

priority.label: priority_2
priority.reason: This supports the key work without carrying the same priority.

schedule.title: Bodyweight Hybrid Circuit
schedule.intent: Primary Hybrid / Hyrox-specific session.

notes: Purpose: Maintain hybrid feel without equipment.
This session is included to improve how well you hold pace and output once fatigue starts building — a major Hybrid / Hyrox limiter.
Execution focus: Keep transitions sharp and mechanics tidy.
Effort guide: 7/10
Useful when equipment is limited but effort still matters.

priority.label: priority_1
priority.reason: This is one of the main goal-driving sessions for the week.

schedule.title: 3 x 10min Threshold
schedule.intent: Longer aerobic development to improve overall endurance.

notes: Purpose: Build sustained threshold capacity with longer controlled reps.
This session plays a specific role in balancing quality, recovery, and performance across the week.
Execution focus: Settle into rhythm early — the last block is where discipline shows.
Effort guide: 7-8/10
Pace guide: based on your submitted 5km, this session should sit around your threshold range of 3:55–4:05/km. Use RPE and control first.
The final rep should be strong, not desperate.

priority.label: priority_2
priority.reason: This supports the key work without carrying the same priority.

schedule.title: Aerobic Support
schedule.intent: Low-cost aerobic support to keep the week balanced.

main: 20–40 min easy Z2 bike or jog
notes: Stay controlled and conversational throughout.

priority.label: priority_3
priority.reason: This adds low-cost aerobic support and can be adjusted if needed.

schedule.title: Recovery / Mobility
schedule.intent: Low-stress recovery support.

notes: Keep this genuinely easy — the goal is to recover well.

priority.label: priority_3
priority.reason: This supports recovery and can be kept easy.

cta.headline: What happens next?
cta.body: This week is built using Hybrid365 principles. If you want to understand how to get the most from it — and how we build real progression — start here.
